does: This paper introduces RMBoost, a synthetic data generation method for training reward models that align large language models with human preferences. Rather than generating two independent responses and then labeling their preference, RMBoost first generates one response, selects a preference label, and then generates a second response conditioned on both the preference label and the first response. The method is evaluated on multiple benchmarks, demonstrating that reward models trained on RMBoost-generated synthetic preference data achieve higher quality than those trained on standard synthetic or human-labeled data, with improved alignment capabilities and reduced reliance on expensive human annotation.
connects: This work builds on the growing literature in reward modeling for LLM alignment, extending prior methods that generate synthetic preference data to improve data quality and efficiency. It addresses limitations of existing approaches like self-play preference generation and simple pairwise response ranking by introducing conditional generation that explicitly targets preference construction. The method relates to broader research on synthetic data generation in language models, preference learning from implicit human feedback, and the challenge of noise in automatically-generated preference labels that has motivated work on data filtering and curriculum learning for RM training.
enables: RMBoost opens opportunities for more scalable and cost-effective reward model development without sacrificing quality, which could accelerate LLM alignment research. The conditional generation approach could inspire similar preference-aware synthesis strategies in other domains requiring pairwise comparisons. It suggests future work on further reducing annotation costs through iterative bootstrapping, generating multi-aspect preference data capturing nuanced human values, and combining synthetic generation with active learning to identify which prompts most benefit from synthetic diversification, ultimately advancing more efficient and human-aligned AI systems.
